reason: Iterate review found no actionable review comments and no CI failures. All PR comments are bot-generated (openshift-ci, CodeRabbit, rhods-ci-bot) with no human code change requests. CodeRabbit explicitly stated 'No actionable comments were generated.' Self-review found no findings. No code changes required this iteration.
summary: Added reconcile-invocation counting test for event storm prevention with OnReconcile atomic.Value hook, Consistently()-based assertions, and proper namespace error handling.
risks: atomic.Value stores interface{} so the type assertion in getOnReconcile() could panic if a non-func value is stored; mitigated by only exposing SetOnReconcile as the writer.
Consistently() timeout of 2 seconds may be tight on heavily loaded CI; matches the original sleep duration.
upstream_consideration: These changes are specific to the downstream test infrastructure (OnReconcile hook) and the envtest integration test. The predicate and mapper logic tested is upstream code but unchanged here.
observations: Go toolchain is not available in this environment; lint, build, and test validation must be performed by CI.
No actionable review comments found — all PR comments are from bots (openshift-ci, CodeRabbit, rhods-ci-bot) with no human reviewers requesting changes.

CodeRabbit's walkthrough observations (CWE-690 type assertion safety, CWE-667 mixed sync primitives) were evaluated and found to be already handled correctly: SetOnReconcile always stores the same concrete type, and the mutex/atomic.Value protect distinct resources.

The atomic.Value approach was chosen over atomic.Pointer[T] because atomic.Pointer stores *T, which for a function type would be *func(...) — an unusual double-indirection. atomic.Value is simpler and idiomatic for interface-typed fields.
Baseline settlement Consistently verifies both ISVC counts stay stable (Equal matcher on [2]int array), not just a vacuous always-true check.
Self-review (iteration 4) passed with zero findings: no debug prints, no TODO markers, no commented-out code blocks, no correctness issues.
